meta.title: The Cow and the Tiger — Vocabulary Set 2
meta.subject: English Vocabulary
meta.description: Week 6 vocabulary focus: named, beautiful, graze, shadowy, forest, angry, caught, sad.

questions.question: The princess wears a _______ dress. It is the most gorgeous dress in the kingdom.
questions.explanation: 'Beautiful' means very pretty or lovely. A gorgeous dress is beautiful — it is visually stunning and impressive.

questions.question: 'Graze' means to eat grass slowly in a field.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'Graze' means to eat grass while walking slowly in a field. Cows and sheep graze — they spend their days walking and eating grass in open fields.

questions.question: We got a new puppy, so we _______ her Coco.
questions.hint: Gave a special name to the puppy
questions.explanation: 'Named' means gave a special name to something. 'We named her Coco' means we chose Coco as the puppy's name.

questions.question: What does 'shadowy' mean?
questions.explanation: 'Shadowy' means dark with little light. A shadowy place has shadows because not much light gets in — like a dark forest or a cave.

questions.question: The cows _______ in the field every morning. They walk and eat the grass.
questions.explanation: 'Graze' means to eat grass slowly. Cows graze in fields — they spend their time walking and eating the grass that grows there.

questions.question: The cave is _______, so I turn on my flashlight so I can see.
questions.explanation: 'Shadowy' means dark with little light. A shadowy cave needs a flashlight because it is too dark to see without one.

questions.question: A 'forest' is a place with many trees growing close together.
questions.explanation: Yes! A 'forest' is a large area of land covered with many trees. In the story, the tiger lived in a shadowy forest — it was dark because the trees blocked the sunlight.

questions.question: He was _______ when his little brother broke his favorite toy.
questions.explanation: 'Angry' means feeling very mad. When something unfair happens, like someone breaking your toy, you feel angry.

questions.question: The boy dropped the ball, but his friend _______ it before it hit the ground.
questions.explanation: 'Caught' means grabbed or held onto something moving. Catching the ball before it hit the ground means grabbing it in time.

questions.question: The child was _______ when she lost her favorite book. She cried a little.
questions.explanation: 'Sad' means feeling unhappy. Losing something you love feels sad — it makes you feel unhappy and want to cry.

questions.question: Sort these Week 6 words by part of speech.
questions.explanation: 'Beautiful,' 'shadowy,' and 'angry' describe things (adjectives). 'Graze' and 'caught' are actions (verbs). 'Forest' is a place (noun).

questions.question: Arrange the words to make a sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: 'The tiger lived in the shadowy forest.' The tiger's home was a dark forest where little sunlight reached through the trees.

questions.question: The opposite of 'beautiful' is:
questions.explanation: The opposite of 'beautiful' (very pretty) is 'ugly' (not attractive). In the story, Chumpa was beautiful — which is the opposite of ugly.

questions.question: 'Named' and 'called' have the same meaning — you can use them interchangeably.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'Named Chumpa' and 'called Chumpa' mean the same thing. 'Named' and 'called' both refer to what something is identified as.

questions.question: Which sentence uses BOTH 'forest' and 'shadowy' correctly?
options: The shadowy forest was a forest shadowy.
It was a shadowy to forest the tiger.
The tiger walked through the shadowy forest, where little sunlight reached.
She named the forest a shadowy caught.
questions.explanation: 'The shadowy forest' — 'shadowy' correctly describes the forest (dark, little light). 'Little sunlight reached' explains why it is shadowy. Both words are used correctly.
